original_image_tags: A newspaper clipping from the "TIMES-HERALD", Washington, D.C., dated Wednesday, April 5, 1950. The clipping is pasted onto the page and has a large red "10" handwritten over the newspaper name.
Handwritten notes in blue pencil reading "file" and an illegible signature next to the index numbers.

elements.description: A newspaper clipping from the Times-Herald, Washington, D.C., dated Wednesday, April 5, 1950. The headline reads 'House Member Swears He Saw Flying Saucer'. The clipping contains typed body text and a sub-headline 'He Saw One'.
A partial newspaper clipping with the sub-headline 'Not Informed' and 'Denials Repeated'. It contains typed body text discussing military and Air Force statements regarding flying saucers.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the existence and basic metadata of the newspaper clipping but disagree on the specific headline text quoted within it, with Description B providing a detailed headline that Description A omits rather than contradicts.
